_meta.spec: W3C Design Tokens Community Group (DTCG) draft

sm.$description: Accent. Use for "focus states" or to imply when something is being "selected"

lg.$description: Very Heavy. Use for heavy branding or for decorative use.

subtle.$description: Transparency layer used onSurface to adapt to background colour.

strong.$description: Transparency layer used onSurface to adapt to background colour.

_.$description: Use as a "raised" area. Or base layer of a component.

transparent.$description: Use when no color is used.

subtle.$description: Transparency layer used onSurface to adapt to background colour.

strong.$description: Transparency layer used onSurface to adapt to background colour.

elevated.$description: Floating Elements: Cards, Modals, Bottom Sheet, etc. Can be used along with shadow effects.

sunken.$description: Used to describe a base Layer or Canvas that is "recessed"

_.$description: Use as a "raised" area. Or base layer of a component.

transparent.$description: Use when no color is used.

elevated.$description: Floating Elements: Cards, Modals, Bottom Sheet, etc. Can be used along with shadow effects.

sunken.$description: Used to describe a base Layer or Canvas that is "recessed"

success.$description: The "inverse" colour has been chosen on purpose to stand out brighter. This colour carries over regardless of light/dark modes.

error.$description: The "inverse" colour has been chosen on purpose to stand out brighter. This colour carries over regardless of light/dark modes.

warning.$description: The "inverse" colour has been chosen on purpose to stand out brighter. This colour carries over regardless of light/dark modes.

brand.$description: The "inverse" colour has been chosen on purpose to stand out brighter. This colour carries over regardless of light/dark modes.

alert.$description: The "inverse" colour has been chosen on purpose to stand out brighter. This colour carries over regardless of light/dark modes.

low.$description: For Overlays or where we need to obscure the screen.

medium.$description: For Overlays or where we need to obscure the screen.

high.$description: For Overlays or where we need to obscure the screen.

subtle.$description: Use as a generic fill layer on top of a colour to mimic hover state where solid colour is not available.

strong.$description: Use as a generic fill layer on top of a colour to mimic hover state where solid colour is not available.

2xs.$description: 10px *This is a NON-standard size and should be extended beyond the original typography scale.

14.$description: *This is a NON-Tailwind Global Class. Please extend the scale to include this.

52.$description: *This is a NON-Tailwind Global Class. Please extend the scale to include this.

76.$description: *This is a NON-Tailwind Global Class. Please extend the scale to include this.

100.$description: *This is a NON-Tailwind Global Class. Please extend the scale to include this.

132.$description: *This is a NON-Tailwind Global Class. Please extend the scale to include this.

loading.$description: *Only use as a layer opacity to describe loading where loading colour token is not appropriate.

disabled.$description: *Only use as a layer opacity to describe loading where loading colour token is not appropriate.

2xs.$description: This is NOT a standard tailwind breakpoint but is important to consider for people using mobile phone web browsers.

xs.$description: This is NOT a standard tailwind breakpoint but is important to consider for people using mobile phone web browsers.

lg.$description: The LAPTOP screen by default in tailwind.config.js

xl.$description: The DESKTOP screen by default in tailwind.config.js

2xl.$description: The breakpoint used for EXTRA WIDE DESKTOP devices

none.$description: Inset Tokens (Internal Padding). These define the space inside a container.
